description_long: This is a vintage gramophone-style record player featuring a large, ornate horn painted in turquoise with gold accents and red interior highlights. The horn is attached to a curved, bronze-colored metal arm that connects to a dark wooden base with decorative paneling. The base houses a circular black turntable platter, upon which a vinyl record is placed, and a visible tonearm with a red stylus head resting on the record. A hand-crank mechanism is present on the side of the base, suggesting manual operation. The overall design is both decorative and functional, evoking early 20th-century aesthetics with a blend of wood, metal, and painted finishes.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large turquoise horn, ornate wooden base, and visible hand-crank and tonearm.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, flared turquoise horn, the circular turntable platter with a record, and the tonearm resting on the record.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length and curve of the horn and tonearm, as well as the hand-crank on the wooden base.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the decorative interior of the horn with red and turquoise patterns and the full circular platter below.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the horn's profile, the tonearm, and the hand-crank on the right side of the base.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and black, occupying the central top area of the wooden base. It appears to be made of plastic or metal, with a smooth, matte finish and a visible vinyl record placed on top. No distinct grooves or patterns are visible on the platter itself.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and curved, extending from the side of the base over the platter. It is bronze-colored with a smooth finish and medium length, positioned so that the stylus head rests on the record.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is visible as a small red component at the end of the tonearm, appearing to be attached and distinct from the rest of the arm. It is small, likely made of plastic or metal, and positioned over the record.
attribute_control_knobs: A single hand-crank is visible on the side of the wooden base, metallic in appearance with a black handle. No other control knobs, switches, or buttons are visible.
